Data Analysis

1. Load Required Library

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

2. Read the CSV File

df = pd.read_csv("data/lightcast_job_postings.csv", low_memory=False)
df.head()
ID LAST_UPDATED_DATE LAST_UPDATED_TIMESTAMP DUPLICATES POSTED EXPIRED DURATION SOURCE_TYPES SOURCES URL ... NAICS_2022_2 NAICS_2022_2_NAME NAICS_2022_3 NAICS_2022_3_NAME NAICS_2022_4 NAICS_2022_4_NAME NAICS_2022_5 NAICS_2022_5_NAME NAICS_2022_6 NAICS_2022_6_NAME

5 rows × 131 columns

3. Check Dataset Shape

print("Rows and Columns:", df.shape)
Rows and Columns: (72498, 131)

4. View Column Names

print(df.columns.tolist())
['ID', 'LAST_UPDATED_DATE', 'LAST_UPDATED_TIMESTAMP', 'DUPLICATES', 'POSTED', 'EXPIRED', 'DURATION', 'SOURCE_TYPES', 'SOURCES', 'URL', 'ACTIVE_URLS', 'ACTIVE_SOURCES_INFO', 'TITLE_RAW', 'BODY', 'MODELED_EXPIRED', 'MODELED_DURATION', 'COMPANY', 'COMPANY_NAME', 'COMPANY_RAW', 'COMPANY_IS_STAFFING', 'EDUCATION_LEVELS', 'EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME', 'MIN_EDULEVELS', 'MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME', 'MAX_EDULEVELS', 'MAX_EDULEVELS_NAME', 'EMPLOYMENT_TYPE', 'EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME', 'MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE', 'MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE', 'IS_INTERNSHIP', 'SALARY', 'REMOTE_TYPE', 'REMOTE_TYPE_NAME', 'ORIGINAL_PAY_PERIOD', 'SALARY_TO', 'SALARY_FROM', 'LOCATION', 'CITY', 'CITY_NAME', 'COUNTY', 'COUNTY_NAME', 'MSA', 'MSA_NAME', 'STATE', 'STATE_NAME', 'COUNTY_OUTGOING', 'COUNTY_NAME_OUTGOING', 'COUNTY_INCOMING', 'COUNTY_NAME_INCOMING', 'MSA_OUTGOING', 'MSA_NAME_OUTGOING', 'MSA_INCOMING', 'MSA_NAME_INCOMING', 'NAICS2', 'NAICS2_NAME', 'NAICS3', 'NAICS3_NAME', 'NAICS4', 'NAICS4_NAME', 'NAICS5', 'NAICS5_NAME', 'NAICS6', 'NAICS6_NAME', 'TITLE', 'TITLE_NAME', 'TITLE_CLEAN', 'SKILLS', 'SKILLS_NAME', 'SPECIALIZED_SKILLS', 'SPECIALIZED_SKILLS_NAME', 'CERTIFICATIONS', 'CERTIFICATIONS_NAME', 'COMMON_SKILLS', 'COMMON_SKILLS_NAME', 'SOFTWARE_SKILLS', 'SOFTWARE_SKILLS_NAME', 'ONET', 'ONET_NAME', 'ONET_2019', 'ONET_2019_NAME', 'CIP6', 'CIP6_NAME', 'CIP4', 'CIP4_NAME', 'CIP2', 'CIP2_NAME', 'SOC_2021_2', 'SOC_2021_2_NAME', 'SOC_2021_3', 'SOC_2021_3_NAME', 'SOC_2021_4', 'SOC_2021_4_NAME', 'SOC_2021_5', 'SOC_2021_5_NAME', 'LOT_CAREER_AREA', 'LOT_CAREER_AREA_NAME', 'LOT_OCCUPATION', 'LOT_OCCUPATION_NAME', 'LOT_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION', 'LOT_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION_NAME', 'LOT_OCCUPATION_GROUP', 'LOT_OCCUPATION_GROUP_NAME', 'LOT_V6_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION', 'LOT_V6_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION_NAME', 'LOT_V6_OCCUPATION', 'LOT_V6_OCCUPATION_NAME', 'LOT_V6_OCCUPATION_GROUP', 'LOT_V6_OCCUPATION_GROUP_NAME', 'LOT_V6_CAREER_AREA', 'LOT_V6_CAREER_AREA_NAME', 'SOC_2', 'SOC_2_NAME', 'SOC_3', 'SOC_3_NAME', 'SOC_4', 'SOC_4_NAME', 'SOC_5', 'SOC_5_NAME', 'LIGHTCAST_SECTORS', 'LIGHTCAST_SECTORS_NAME', 'NAICS_2022_2', 'NAICS_2022_2_NAME', 'NAICS_2022_3', 'NAICS_2022_3_NAME', 'NAICS_2022_4', 'NAICS_2022_4_NAME', 'NAICS_2022_5', 'NAICS_2022_5_NAME', 'NAICS_2022_6', 'NAICS_2022_6_NAME']

8 rows × 38 columns

6. Null values

# Calculate count and percentage (multiplied by 100)
null_summary = pd.concat([df.isnull().sum(), (df.isnull().mean() * 100)],axis=1)
# Rename columns for clarity
null_summary.columns = ['null_count', 'null_percentage']
missing_sorted = null_summary[null_summary['null_count'] > 0].sort_values('null_percentage', ascending=False)
print(missing_sorted)
                      null_count  null_percentage
ACTIVE_SOURCES_INFO        64680        89.216254
MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE       64068        88.372093
MAX_EDULEVELS_NAME         56183        77.495931
MAX_EDULEVELS              56183        77.495931
LIGHTCAST_SECTORS          54711        75.465530
...                          ...              ...
URL                           22         0.030346
POSTED                        22         0.030346
SOURCE_TYPES                  22         0.030346
SOURCES                       22         0.030346
DUPLICATES                    22         0.030346

[131 rows x 2 columns]

7. Box Plot (Salary)

df.boxplot(column='SALARY', by='NAICS2_NAME', vert=False)
plt.show()

8. Sector Wise Salary

result = df.groupby('NAICS2_NAME').agg(
    max_value=('SALARY', 'max'),
    min_value=('SALARY', 'min'),
    avg_value=('SALARY', 'mean')
)

print(result)
                                                    max_value  min_value  \
NAICS2_NAME                                                                
Accommodation and Food Services                      338750.0    20800.0   
Administrative and Support and Waste Management...   500000.0    27040.0   
Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing and Hunting           222000.0    52800.0   
Arts, Entertainment, and Recreation                  180000.0    33176.0   
Construction                                         194500.0    31387.0   
Educational Services                                 262250.0    22440.0   
Finance and Insurance                                311000.0    31200.0

Manufacturing                                        319100.0    26520.0   
Mining, Quarrying, and Oil and Gas Extraction        185750.0    49920.0

Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services     312500.0    23585.0

Real Estate and Rental and Leasing                   260000.0    28080.0

Transportation and Warehousing                       195000.0    27300.0   
Unclassified Industry                                372500.0    15860.0   
Utilities                                            234000.0    39020.0   
Wholesale Trade                                      350000.0    31200.0   

                                                        avg_value  
NAICS2_NAME                                                        
Accommodation and Food Services                     145674.501916  
Administrative and Support and Waste Management...  102942.477296  
Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing and Hunting           98591.857143  
Arts, Entertainment, and Recreation                  91034.790698

Manufacturing                                       122408.817088  
Mining, Quarrying, and Oil and Gas Extraction       103722.861111

Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services    132601.547266

Transportation and Warehousing                      100463.898678  
Unclassified Industry                               108802.251995

9. Clean Data

df_salary = df.copy()

df_salary = df_salary[
    (df_salary['SALARY_FROM'].notna()) &
    (df_salary['SALARY_TO'].notna())
]

df_salary['avg_salary'] = (
    df_salary['SALARY_FROM'] + df_salary['SALARY_TO']
) / 2

10. AI vs Non AI

ai_keywords = ['data scientist', 'machine learning', 'ai', 'artificial intelligence', 'nlp']

df_salary['is_ai'] = df_salary['TITLE_NAME'].str.lower().apply(
    lambda x: any(keyword in str(x) for keyword in ai_keywords)
)

ai_vs_nonai = df_salary.groupby('is_ai')['avg_salary'].mean()
print(ai_vs_nonai)
is_ai
False    114982.178802
True     119120.349917
Name: avg_salary, dtype: float64

11. AI vs Non AI-Plot

ai_vs_nonai.plot(kind='bar')
plt.title('AI vs Non-AI Salary Comparison')
plt.ylabel('Average Salary')
plt.xticks([0,1], ['Non-AI', 'AI'], rotation=0)
plt.show()

12. Region Wise - Salary

region_salary = df_salary.groupby('STATE_NAME')['avg_salary'].mean().sort_values(ascending=False)

print(region_salary.head(10))
STATE_NAME
Connecticut                                124878.200466
New Jersey                                 122861.899888
Arkansas                                   122166.234076
Vermont                                    121737.644860
Virginia                                   121207.365425
California                                 120828.784724
Montana                                    120698.048246
Illinois                                   120415.422669
Washington                                 119686.824056
Washington, D.C. (District of Columbia)    118605.085859
Name: avg_salary, dtype: float64

13. Region Wise - Salary Plot

region_salary.head(10).plot(kind='bar')
plt.title('Top 10 States by Salary')
plt.ylabel('Average Salary')
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()

14. Remote VS Onsite

remote_salary = df_salary.groupby('REMOTE_TYPE_NAME')['avg_salary'].mean()
print(remote_salary)
REMOTE_TYPE_NAME
Hybrid Remote    114107.098782
Not Remote       100698.898251
Remote           116455.761715
[None]           115079.057876
Name: avg_salary, dtype: float64

15. Remote VS Onsite - Plot

remote_salary.plot(kind='bar')
plt.title('Remote vs Onsite Salary')
plt.ylabel('Average Salary')
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()

16. Skills

from collections import Counter

skills_series = df['SKILLS_NAME'].dropna().str.split(';')
all_skills = [skill.strip() for sublist in skills_series for skill in sublist]

skill_counts = Counter(all_skills)

top_skills = pd.DataFrame(skill_counts.most_common(7), columns=['Skill', 'Count'])
print(top_skills)

top_skills.set_index('Skill').plot(kind='bar')
plt.title('Top In-Demand Skills')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.xticks(rotation=90)
plt.show()
                                               Skill  Count
0                                                 []    523
1  [\n  "User Experience (UX) Design",\n  "Projec...    376
2  [\n  "Cloud Computing",\n  "Enterprise Resourc...    279
3  [\n  "Pivot Tables And Charts",\n  "Business O...    270
4                            [\n  "Data Analysis"\n]    152
5  [\n  "Management",\n  "Microsoft Access",\n  "...    112
6  [\n  "Customer Service",\n  "Emergency Respons...    110
